evidence.text: , but the model had improved substantially on longer tasks a controlled experiment would have surfaced. Miscalibration runs both ways. Without offline-online signal calibration, our evals are opinions, not evidence. By construction, long-running tasks and long-term behaviour are challenging to capture with evals. By continuously adjusting the evals to improve their mapping to online outcomes, the evals are becoming better and better verification tools. We are not ruling out that in the future, as AI develops, evals can map well enough to start acting as validations : By having the offline/online calibration loop in place we have continuous transparency on what role evals
